name: Law Office of Simon Goldenberg, PLLC
description_short: New York and New Jersey debt relief law firm specializing in debt settlement, bankruptcy alternatives, and consumer debt collection defense led by Attorney Simon Goldenberg.
description_long:
The Law Office of Simon Goldenberg, PLLC is a consumer advocacy and debt relief law firm based in New York that serves clients in New York and New Jersey. Attorney Simon Goldenberg has been recognized with an Avvo Clients' Choice Award and selected as a Rising Star by SuperLawyers for 2014-2022, indicating peer recognition within the legal community.

The firm offers strategic debt negotiation and settlement services as alternatives to bankruptcy, particularly for credit card and student loan debts. They provide lawsuit defense for clients facing debt collection actions, conduct free initial evaluations to assess client situations, and help clients compare bankruptcy versus debt settlement options. The firm emphasizes protection of consumers from abusive debt collection practices and works to defend client rights under the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act (FDCPA) and Fair Credit Reporting Act provisions.

The firm distinguishes itself through personalized, customized action plans for each client rather than standardized approaches. They claim to combine aggressive negotiation tactics with individual attention, positioning themselves against the "teams of lawyers" that banks employ. Client testimonials highlight fast resolutions (settlement within a week mentioned), cost savings, and assistance with student loan issues specifically.

As a law firm rather than a debt settlement company, this represents a hybrid model—they appear to practice consumer law with debt relief as a specialty, rather than being a traditional debt settlement firm. Clients should understand they are hiring legal representation, which typically costs more than non-legal debt settlement services but may provide stronger legal protections, particularly in lawsuit defense scenarios.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

tiers.name: Debt Settlement Program
features: Creditor negotiation for reduced payoff amounts
Monthly deposit into dedicated savings account

pros: Attorney recognized with Avvo Clients' Choice Award and SuperLawyers Rising Star selection (2014-2022)
Offers free initial consultations and evaluations before committing to services
Handles debt settlement for both credit cards and student loans, not just one debt type
Provides lawsuit defense services for clients facing collection actions, not just settlement
Explicitly focuses on protecting consumers from abusive debt collection practices under FDCPA
Testimonials document rapid resolutions (settlement achieved within one week in at least one case)
Customized action plans per client rather than standardized debt relief programs
cons: Limited geographic service area (New York and New Jersey only)
No pricing information disclosed on website; law firm rates typically higher than non-legal debt settlement companies
No information on success rates, average settlement percentages, or typical timeframes
No details on how they differ from non-legal debt settlement firms or what legal advantages they provide
Reliance on client testimonials without third-party verification; some testimonials are generic praise
best_for: New York and New Jersey residents facing collection lawsuits who need legal defense
Consumers with mixed debt types (credit cards and student loans) seeking consolidated legal assistance
Debtors experiencing abusive collection practices who need FDCPA protection and enforcement
Individuals unsure whether bankruptcy or settlement is appropriate who want attorney guidance to compare options
diagnosis: Best for New York/New Jersey residents who are being sued by debt collectors or who prefer legal representation over non-legal debt settlement companies. Main caveat: This is a law firm charging attorney rates, not a traditional debt settlement company—clients should clarify fee structures and understand whether the legal expertise justifies costs compared to alternatives, particularly if they're not facing active litigation. Consumers evaluating debt relief companies should also consider whether debt consolidation loans, credit counseling, or personal loans for bad credit might provide a better path to financial recovery depending on their specific situation.
services: Strategic debt negotiation and settlement for credit cards
Student loan debt settlement and negotiation
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
